name: California (United States)
description: A white field with a wide red stripe along the bottom edge. In the centre, a brown California grizzly bear walks on a small patch of green grass; a red five-pointed star appears in the upper hoist, and the words 'CALIFORNIA REPUBLIC' are printed in black between the bear and the red stripe.
history: The design is based on the original Bear Flag raised at Sonoma on 14 June 1846 during the short-lived Bear Flag Revolt against Mexican rule. The original was made of unbleached cotton with a hand-drawn bear and star and burned in the 1906 San Francisco earthquake. The Bear Flag was adopted as the official state flag by the California State Legislature on 3 February 1911; the present standardized design — including the bear modeled on Monarch, the last captive California grizzly — was codified in 1953.

features.symbolism: strength and unyielding resistance; the now-extinct California grizzly bear (Ursus arctos californicus) walking on a green grass plot
sovereignty, after the Lone Star Flag of 1836

features.role: the words 'CALIFORNIA REPUBLIC' beneath the bear
